steps.title: Identify the temporal contrast
steps.content: Neon was discovered and isolated in the same year. Erbium was isolated 91 years after its discovery. This is a clear temporal contrast between a quick process and a prolonged one.

steps.title: Match the temporal contrast to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast. Neon was isolated the same year it was discovered (fast); erbium took 91 years (slow). The blank must signal this direct opposition.
  • Choice A — "For instance,": signals that erbium is a specific example of the neon pattern — but erbium is the opposite case, not an example of it, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "By contrast,": directly flags the opposition between neon's rapid isolation and erbium's 91-year delay, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "Thus,": signals that erbium's slow isolation follows as a consequence of neon's fast isolation — no causal link exists, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "Similarly,": signals resemblance, but erbium's timeline is the opposite of neon's, so fails. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
